title: Rekindle Enterprise Policy
description: JSON Schema for the canonical enterprise-policy document Rekindle reads at startup. Modelled on Mozilla Firefox `policies.json` (flat keys, JSON value types). Distribution: signed bundle on disk OR OS-native policy store (Windows registry / macOS managed preferences / Linux config). See docs/contributor/enterprise-policy.md for the full reference.

$schema.description: Reserved — schema URL for tooling. Ignored by the loader.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Bumped when breaking changes ship.

metadata.description: Bundle-level metadata. Display only — does not affect policy resolution.

organization.description: Organisation name shown in 'Managed by …' hints.

contactEmail.description: Admin contact for policy questions; surfaced in the policy-audit window.

supportUrl.description: URL surfaced in the policy-audit window.

expiresAt.description: Bundle expiry date. After this, the loader treats the policy as advisory and shows a warning.

policies.description: The policy keys. Every key is optional — unset keys fall through to the application default.

_comment.description: Optional admin comment. Ignored by the loader.

_lock.description: List of policy keys that are MANDATORY (cannot be overridden by user preference). Every key in this list MUST also have a value set in `policies`. Keys not in this list are RECOMMENDED — applied as defaults but the user can override.

Identity.requireKeypairVerification.description: Block sign-in unless the local identity keypair has been verified out-of-band against an admin-provided fingerprint.

Identity.allowedFingerprintList.description: Whitelist of identity-key fingerprints that are allowed to use this Rekindle install. Empty list means no whitelist.

Identity.maxOfflineHours.description: Force re-auth after N hours offline. 0 = never re-auth (default).

Identity.disableMultipleIdentities.description: Restrict the install to a single Rekindle identity. Useful for managed devices where users should only use the corporate identity.

Federation.allowedBootstrapNodes.description: Veilid bootstrap node addresses that the daemon may use. Empty list means use the network defaults. Locks Rekindle to a private Veilid mesh when populated.

Federation.outboundProxyUrl.description: SOCKS5 / HTTP proxy URL for non-Veilid egress (e.g., link-preview OpenGraph fetches). Honour `none` to disable that egress entirely.

Federation.disableLinkPreview.description: Disable OpenGraph link previews (suppresses external HTTP egress for link metadata).

Federation.disablePushRelay.description: Forbid enrolment with any mobile push relay. The Tier-3 wake-notify channel is unavailable when set.

Communities.allowCreate.description: Permit the user to create new communities.

Communities.allowedJoinList.description: Whitelist of community DHT keys the user may join. Empty list means no whitelist (any community is joinable).

Communities.blockedJoinList.description: Deny-list of community DHT keys. Joining one of these is refused at the IPC boundary.

Communities.mandatoryList.description: Communities the user is auto-joined into on first login. Display-only signal in the UI; does NOT bypass community-side governance — the user still needs an invite.

Messaging.maxFileSizeBytes.description: Hard cap on individual file size for outbound attachments.

Messaging.blockedFileExtensions.description: Deny-list of file extensions for outbound attachments. Lower-case, no leading dot. Example: ['exe', 'scr', 'msi'].

Messaging.disableMarkdown.description: Render messages as plain text only. Disables markdown bold / italic / code-fence rendering.

Messaging.mandatoryDisclaimer.description: Text displayed once per session in every conversation pane (e.g., legal-required disclaimer).

Messaging.localRetentionDays.description: Local message-history retention in days. 0 = unlimited (default). After this, local SQLite rows are pruned. Note: peers retain their own copies — this is a *local* policy.

Voice.allowScreenShare.description: Permit screen sharing in voice calls.

Voice.muteOnJoin.description: Auto-mute microphone on entering a voice channel / call.

Voice.disableRecording.description: Refuse to start a local recording. Scope: client UI only; cannot prevent remote participants from recording (they're on their own clients).

Voice.maxCallDurationMinutes.description: Force-end calls after N minutes. 0 = unlimited (default).

Privacy.disableGameDetection.description: Disable the cross-platform process scanner and rich-presence game-info field.

Privacy.hidePresence.description: Suppress presence-status broadcast (online/away/busy). Honours Rekindle's privacy posture: presence is only visible to peers anyway, but disabling it removes one metadata channel.

Privacy.disableTelemetry.description: Reserved. Rekindle ships zero telemetry by design — this key is documented so admins can pin the property explicitly.

Update.checkIntervalHours.description: How often to poll for updates.

Update.minimumVersion.description: Refuse to start the app if the installed version is older than this. Forces auto-upgrade flow.

Update.deferralMaxDays.description: Maximum days a user may defer a pending update before it is enforced.

UI.organisationName.description: Organisation name surfaced in the 'Managed by …' hint and the policy-audit window.

UI.organisationLogoUrl.description: HTTPS URL of an organisation logo to display in the buddy-list header. Must be HTTPS.

UI.loginMotd.description: Message-of-the-day shown on the login window.

UI.helpUrl.description: URL the in-app 'Help' link points to (typically the org's IT support portal).

UI.disableLaunchToTray.description: Force the app to start as a visible window rather than hidden in the system tray.

Audit.localLogRetentionDays.description: How long to retain client-side audit log entries on disk. 0 = no audit log written.

Audit.logSignInEvents.description: Record sign-in attempts (success + failure) to the local audit log.

Audit.logCommunityEvents.description: Record community join/leave/role-change events to the local audit log.

Audit.logCallMetadata.description: Record call start/end/participant-count to the local audit log. METADATA ONLY — no audio, no transcript, no message bodies. Anything else would conflict with E2E (see policy-trust-model.md §3).

Audit.shipEndpointUrl.description: Optional HTTPS endpoint to ship audit-log entries to. METADATA-ONLY scope is enforced at the source: no ciphertext, no plaintext payload, no peer pseudonyms. The user is shown a 'logs are forwarded to <endpoint>' notice in the audit window. Set to empty string to disable shipping.
